title: Erase-then-Delta Attention: Decoupling Erase and Write Addresses in Delta-Rule Linear Attention
abstract: arXiv:2606.26560v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Delta-rule linear attention improves recurrent memory updates by correcting what is already stored at the current write address before writing new content. However, the active correction is still anchored to that same write address. As a result, stale information stored at a different address cannot be actively removed before new content is written elsewhere. We propose Erase-then-Delta Attention (EDA), a memory update rule that decouples where to erase from where to write. The key insight is that recurrent memory models should not only correct the current write, but also selectively suppress outdated memory at an independently chosen address. Concretely, our method first applies a targeted erase step along a learned erase direction, and then performs the standard delta-style corrective write along the current write direction. This preserves the corrective behavior of delta-rule updates while expanding their memory-management capacity. L

Title: Erase-then-Delta Attention: Decoupling Erase and Write Addresses in Delta-Rule Linear Attention
Abstract: arXiv:2606.26560v1 Announce Type: new  Abstract: Delta-rule linear attention improves recurrent memory updates by correcting what is already stored at the current write address before writing new content. However, the active correction is still anchored to that same write address. As a result, stale information stored at a different address cannot be actively removed before new content is written elsewhere. We propose Erase-then-Delta Attention (EDA), a memory update rule that decouples where to erase from where to write. The key insight is that recurrent memory models should not only correct the current write, but also selectively suppress outdated memory at an independently chosen address. Concretely, our method first applies a targeted erase step along a learned erase direction, and then performs the standard delta-style corrective write along the current write direction. This preserves the corrective behavior of delta-rule updates while expanding their memory-management capacity. L
translated_title: 擦除-然后-增量注意力：在增量规则线性注意力中解耦擦除与写入地址
translated_abstract: arXiv:2606.26560v1 公告类型：新  摘要：增量规则线性注意力通过先纠正当前写入地址中已存储的内容再写入新信息，改进了循环记忆更新。然而，这种主动修正仍然局限于同一个写入地址。因此，当新内容被写入其他位置时，存储在不同地址的过时信息无法被主动移除。我们提出了擦除-然后-增量注意力（EDA），一种将擦除地址与写入地址解耦的记忆更新规则。其关键见解在于：循环记忆模型不仅应纠正当前的写入，还应在一个独立选择的地址上选择性地抑制过时的记忆。具体而言，我们的方法首先沿着一个学习到的擦除方向执行针对性的擦除步骤，然后沿着当前写入方向执行标准的增量式修正写入。这保留了增量规则更新的修正行为，同时扩展了其记忆管理能力。
tldr: 提出擦除-然后-增量注意力（EDA），通过将擦除地址与写入地址解耦，在增量规则线性注意力中实现对过时记忆的主动抑制，从而扩展记忆管理能力。
en_tdlr: Propose Erase-then-Delta Attention (EDA), which decouples erase addresses from write addresses in delta-rule linear attention to actively suppress outdated memory, thereby expanding memory management capacity.
